questions.answer: This president, shot during a campaign speech in 1912, refused to go to the hospital and finished his 90-minute address before seeking medical help.
In 1974, this daredevil attempted to jump the Snake River Canyon in Idaho on a rocket-powered 'Skycycle.'
This boxer, who famously said 'Float like a butterfly, sting like a bee,' shocked the world in 1964 by defeating Sonny Liston as a massive underdog to claim the heavyweight title.
This Cuban-born revolutionary crossed the Atlantic in 1965 to help lead guerrilla uprisings in Africa and Bolivia, abandoning a cushy government post to keep fighting.
This Viking explorer is widely credited as the first European to reach North America, doing so nearly 500 years before Columbus.
This 1994 film follows an ex-con named Andy Dufresne who befriends a fellow prisoner named Red while serving time at a prison.
Tom Hanks delivered the line 'Life is like a box of chocolates' in this 1994 Best Picture winner.
This director's credits include Jaws, E.T., Schindler's List, and the Indiana Jones franchise.
The 1980 horror film where Jack Nicholson's character types 'All work and no play makes Jack a dull boy' over and over was directed by Stanley Kubrick.
The 1941 Orson Welles film widely considered the greatest ever made centers on a reporter investigating the life of the fictional media mogul Charles Foster Kane.
This dark Irish stout, brewed since 1759, is famous for its creamy head that drinkers have made a game out of called "Splitting the G".
IPAs get their bitter, hoppy punch from this cone-shaped flowering plant, also used as a natural preservative in beer.
This Pacific Northwest city is home to more craft breweries per capita than almost anywhere in the U.S., earning the nickname 'Beervana.'
This term describes a beer fermented at cooler temperatures using bottom-fermenting yeast, producing a crisper, cleaner flavor than its ale counterpart.
Brewers use this term for the thick, sugary liquid produced after mashing grain — before hops are added and fermentation begins, it tastes something like unfiltered barley tea.
This iconic 1980 Namco arcade game was so popular in Japan that it reportedly caused a national shortage of 100-yen coins.
Atari's 1972 table tennis arcade game is widely considered the first commercially successful video game.
This handheld Nintendo console launched in 1989 and turned the Game Boy into a cultural phenomenon with a certain block-stacking puzzle game.
The original Xbox launched in 2001 alongside this sci-fi shooter franchise, which became the console's killer app and remains its flagship series.
This 1999 N64 game featuring Link was the first 3D entry in its franchise and is often cited as the highest-rated video game ever reviewed.
Paris Hilton made this two-word phrase her signature catchphrase, often said while posing for cameras in the mid-2000s.
This video-sharing website, launched in 2005, hosted the very first upload — a clip of co-founder Jawed Karim at the zoo.
Released in 2001, this Apple device came with a scroll wheel and was marketed with the slogan '1,000 songs in your pocket.'
This rapper's 2003 debut album 'Get Rich or Die Tryin'' sold one million copies in its first four days, the fastest-selling debut in music history at the time.
In 2007, this social networking site overtook MySpace in U.S. unique monthly visitors, marking a definitive shift in the social media landscape.
This Australian duo's 1981 hit 'Down Under' is an iconic anthem about Vegemite sandwiches and wandering — their only U.S. top 40 entry.
Los Del Rio's 1996 line-dance craze, was sparked by this song which spent 14 weeks at #1, making it one of the longest-running #1 singles ever.
This one-named Norwegian singer topped charts worldwide in 1997 with the bouncy Europop debut of "Barbie Girl." Her follow-up singles barely caused a ripple.
This 'Ice Ice Baby' rapper was the first hip-hop artist to top the Billboard Hot 100, but his follow-up singles never cracked the top 40 again.
This band's 1982 single 'Tainted Love' reached #1 in 17 countries, yet they never cracked the U.S. top 40 again despite releasing albums throughout the decade.
The Wizard of Oz ends with Dorothy clicking her heels and repeating this six-word phrase three times before waking up in Kansas.
questions.correct_question: What is 'There's no place like home'?

questions.answer: After being fatally shot at the Ford Theatre, this President’s last recorded words were reportedly a response to his wife, saying, "She won't think anything about it."
Julius Caesar's last words, as written by Shakespeare, were spoken in Latin and accusingly addressed to this man.
This prolific inventor and founder of GE reportedly came out of a coma, looked at his wife, and said, "It is very beautiful over there."
Nathan Hale's famous final statement before his 1776 execution expressed regret that he had only one of these to give for his country.
Winston Churchill was famously devoted to this bubbly French wine, once saying 'in victory, I deserve it; in defeat, I need it.'
Don McLean drove his Chevy to the levee, but the levee was dry — yet 'the good ol' boys were drinking whiskey and rye' in this 1971 epic song.
The 1969 Woodstock festival's original poster promised 'An Aquarian Exposition' — and while the music was free, festivalgoers paid $1.50 for a cup of this fermented apple drink.
Ben Franklin is often misquoted as calling this drink 'proof that God loves us,' but he actually wrote that line about wine in a letter to André Morellet.
The 1814 London Beer Flood, in which a brewery tank ruptured and killed eight people, involved this specific style of dark ale.

final_jeopardy.answer: It's the only internal organ in the human body capable of completely regenerating itself — regrowing to full size even after up to 75% has been removed.
